paper_title: Plankton Sample Collection and Analysis in the Mediterranean Sea

methods.description: Collection of plankton samples from the Mediterranean Sea using a plankton pump system for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Surface water was continuously pumped on board from 5m water depth and lead through a plankton net with 100 μm mesh size.
steps.searchable_summary: pump surface water through plankton net

steps.description: Replacing the cod-end every 6h, accumulated samples were washed out of the net into a 90 μm sieve, rinsed thoroughly with deionized water to remove smaller particles as well as salts, and subsequently stored onboard at -80°C.

methods.description: Preparation of plankton samples for isotope analysis by freeze-drying and low temperature ashing.

steps.description: At NIOZ all plankton samples were then freeze-dried, and dry oxidation by low temperature ashing (100°C) was used to combust the organic material while minimizing potential impacts on carbonate trace metal concentrations and δ 18 O.
After ashing, samples were rinsed again thoroughly with de-ionized water and ethanol to remove potential ash residues.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse ashed samples with water and ethanol

methods.description: Measurement of elemental ratios in foraminiferal shells using laser ablation quadrupole inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (LA-Q-ICP-MS).

steps.description: The elemental ratio of the final foraminiferal chamber (named the F-chamber) of individual shells were measured by LA-Q-ICP-MS using a circular spot with a diameter of 60-80 µm.
Masses 7Li, 11B,23Na,24Mg,25Mg,27Al,43Ca,44Ca,57Fe, 88Sr, 137Ba and 238U were monitored, 44Ca served as an internal standard for quantification of the associated elements.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor specific masses for elemental analysis

methods.description: Measurement of stable oxygen and carbon isotopes in foraminiferal calcite using an automated carbonate device connected to an isotope ratio mass spectrometer.

steps.description: Stable oxygen and carbon isotopes of foraminiferal calcite were measured on groups of whole specimens using an automated carbonate device connected to Thermo Finnigan MAT 253 Dual Inlet Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometer.
Due to the large amount of material required, specimens from different samples sometimes needed to be combined.

methods.description: Computation of the likely provenance of foraminifera by backtracking virtual particles in a high-resolution ocean model.

steps.description: The likely provenance of the foraminifera sampled was computed by backtracking virtual particles in a high-resolution ocean model using the Copernicus Marine Environmental Monitoring Service (CMEMS) Global Reanalysis model.
We released 10,000 particles equally spaced between the start and end locations of 25 of the transects and tracked the particles back for 30 days with a 4th order Runge-Kutta algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: release and track particles using Runge-Kutta
